report_name: Unit 42 Discloses Universal Bucket Hijacking Flaw Affecting AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The bucket hijacking technique requires an attacker to delete the legitimate cloud storage bucket (`s3:DeleteBucket` or `storage.bucket.delete`), making the globally unique name available for the attacker to reclaim.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: After hijacking the bucket name, existing data streams automatically begin routing all new data to the attacker's bucket, which is located in a separate, attacker-controlled cloud account, resulting in silent data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attack requires initial access to the victim's cloud environment via compromised IAM credentials to obtain the necessary permissions for bucket manipulation.

attack_patterns.name: Domain or Tenant Policy Modification
attack_patterns.description: To execute the attack, an adversary may need to escalate privileges by modifying IAM policies to grant themselves bucket deletion permissions, such as `storage.bucket.delete` or `s3:DeleteBucket`.

vulnerabilities.name: Cloud Storage Global Namespace Hijacking
vulnerabilities.description: An architectural vulnerability in major cloud platforms where the global uniqueness of bucket names can be exploited. An attacker with permissions to delete a bucket can recreate it under their own account, hijacking any data streams pointing to that name to silently exfiltrate data.

identities.name: Unit 42
identities.description: The security research group from Palo Alto Networks that discovered and disclosed the bucket hijacking vulnerability.

identities.name: Palo Alto Networks
identities.description: The parent company of Unit 42, which disclosed the bucket hijacking vulnerability.

identities.name: Amazon Web Services
identities.description: A major cloud service provider whose S3 service is affected by the bucket hijacking vulnerability.

identities.name: Google Cloud
identities.description: A major cloud service provider whose Cloud Storage service is affected by the bucket hijacking vulnerability.

identities.name: Microsoft Azure
identities.description: A major cloud service provider whose Blob Storage service is affected by the bucket hijacking vulnerability.

infrastructure.name: Hijacked Cloud Storage Bucket
infrastructure.description: A cloud storage bucket recreated by an attacker with the same name as a legitimate bucket to intercept data streams, as demonstrated in Unit 42's research.

indicators.name: GCP Storage Bucket Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: GCP Storage Bucket Deletion
id: 7f1b2c3d-4e5f-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects the deletion of a Google Cloud Storage bucket, which could be a precursor to a bucket hijacking attack.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-bucket-hijacking-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-23
tags:
  - attack.t1485
level: high
logsource:
  product: gcp
  service: gcp.audit
detection:
  selection:
    gcp.audit.method_name: 'storage.buckets.delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the deletion of a Google Cloud Storage bucket, a key step in the bucket hijacking attack.

indicators.name: AWS S3 Bucket Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: AWS S3 Bucket Deletion
id: 8a2c3d4e-5f6a-4891-8cde-f12345678901
status: experimental
description: Detects the deletion of an AWS S3 bucket via the DeleteBucket API call, which could be a precursor to a bucket hijacking attack.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-bucket-hijacking-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-23
tags:
  - attack.t1485
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventSource: 's3.amazonaws.com'
    eventName: 'DeleteBucket'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the deletion of an AWS S3 bucket, a key step in the bucket hijacking attack.

indicators.name: Azure Blob Container Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Azure Blob Container Deletion
id: 9b3d4e5f-6a7b-4012-8def-123456789012
status: experimental
description: Detects the deletion of an Azure Blob Storage container, which could be a precursor to a bucket hijacking attack.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-bucket-hijacking-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-23
tags:
  - attack.t1485
level: high
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: activitylogs
detection:
  selection:
    operationName|endswith: 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/delete'
    category: 'Administrative'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the deletion of an Azure Blob Storage container, a key step in the bucket hijacking attack.

indicators.name: Cloud Storage Bucket Deletion via CLI
indicators.pattern:
title: Cloud Storage Bucket Deletion via CLI
id: ad4e5f6a-7b8c-4123-8ef0-234567890123
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line execution of tools used to delete cloud storage buckets.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-bucket-hijacking-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-23
tags:
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.t1059
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_gcloud:
    Image|endswith: '\gcloud.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'storage'
      - 'buckets'
      - 'delete'
  selection_aws:
    Image|endswith: '\aws.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 's3api'
      - 'delete-bucket'
  condition: 1 of them
indicators.description: Detects the use of `gcloud` or `aws` command-line tools to delete a storage bucket.

relationships.source_name: Cloud Storage Bucket Deletion via CLI
